title: The Future of Work
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The (0) automation of many routine tasks is transforming the modern workplace. Technology is creating new areas of (17) employment for skilled workers, though a (18) significant number of traditional jobs are also disappearing. Future employees will need to show (19) adaptability and qualities such as (20) creativity and critical thinking.||Many companies now offer greater (21) flexibility to attract talented staff, while individuals are increasingly seeking (22) professional development opportunities to stay relevant. Governments must also take (23) responsibility for retraining workers who have been affected. Investing in (24) education is widely seen as the best way to prepare people for the changing world of work.

example.text: The (0) AUTOMATION of many routine tasks is transforming the modern workplace. (AUTOMATE)

questions.explanation: The noun 'employment' is formed from the verb 'employ' by adding the suffix '-ment'.
The adjective 'significant' is formed from 'signify' by changing '-ify' to '-icant'.
The noun 'adaptability' is formed from 'adapt' by adding the suffix '-ability'.
The noun 'creativity' is formed from 'create' by replacing '-e' with the suffix '-ivity'.
The noun 'flexibility' is formed from 'flexible' by changing '-ble' to '-bility'.
The adjective 'professional' is formed from 'profession' by adding the suffix '-al'.
The noun 'responsibility' is formed from 'responsible' by changing '-ble' to '-bility'.
The noun 'education' is formed from 'educate' by changing '-ate' to '-ation'.
